title: Online Clipboard — Share Text Instantly | Webklip
description: Free online clipboard for instant text sharing. No sign-up, real-time sync, automatic expiry. Create a web clipboard in one click.
intro: Copy text on one device and paste it on another — without email, chat apps, or cloud storage accounts.
paragraphs: An online clipboard is a web-based tool that stores text temporarily so you can access it from any browser. Webklip turns any URL into a shared clipboard: paste your content, send the link, and open it on your phone, laptop, or tablet.

Unlike system clipboards that only work on one machine, a web clipboard syncs in real time. Type on your desktop and see the same text appear on your phone seconds later. When you are done, the clip expires automatically — no leftover copies in your message history.
Webklip is free, requires no account, and works in any modern browser. It is the simplest way to share text online when you just need to move something between devices quickly.
benefits: No sign-up — create a clip and share the link immediately
Real-time sync across all open devices
Automatic deletion after expiry
Works on phone, tablet, and desktop browsers
useCases: Copy a shopping list from laptop to phone
Share a URL or address with yourself
Move meeting notes between workstations

title: Share Text Between Devices — Copy Instantly | Webklip
description: Copy text between phone and desktop instantly. Free web clipboard with real-time sync. No app install required.
intro: Stop emailing yourself. Copy between devices with a single link that syncs in real time.
paragraphs: Moving text between your phone and computer should take seconds, not minutes. Webklip lets you copy between devices by creating a temporary web page that acts as a shared clipboard. Paste on one device, open the link on another, and your content is there.

Real-time synchronization means you do not even need to refresh. Keep both devices open and watch text appear as you type. This makes Webklip ideal for quick transfers — a code snippet, a phone number, a paragraph you are editing — without installing anything.
Every clip expires on a schedule you control, from 15 minutes to 7 days. Your text does not sit in a chat thread or email inbox forever. It is a disposable clipboard built for the moment.
benefits: Instant text sharing with no app install
Real-time updates as you type on any device
Configurable expiry from 15 minutes to 7 days
Optional PIN or encryption for sensitive transfers
useCases: Transfer a verification code from phone to laptop
Continue writing a paragraph on a different device
Share a long URL without retyping it

title: Temporary File Sharing — Send Files Online | Webklip
description: Share files online temporarily without permanent cloud links. Attach images and PDFs to clips that auto-expire.
intro: Send a file for a few hours, not forever. Attach to a clip that deletes itself when done.
paragraphs: Most file sharing tools create permanent links that linger in cloud storage for years. Webklip takes a different approach: attach files to a temporary clip that expires on a schedule. Share an image, PDF, or document with a single URL, and let it disappear when you no longer need it.

File attachments work alongside text content in the same clip. Paste a note, attach a screenshot, and send one link. Recipients open it in their browser — no account, no download folder full of old transfers.

Automatic cleanup removes expired clips and their files on a schedule. You do not need to remember to delete anything. Temporary file sharing should be temporary, and Webklip makes sure it stays that way.
benefits: Attach images, PDFs, and documents to any clip
Files deleted automatically when the clip expires
No permanent cloud storage account required
Combine text and files in a single shareable link
useCases: Send a screenshot for a quick review
Share a PDF for a one-time download
Transfer a document between phone and desktop

title: Secure Clipboard — PIN & Encryption | Webklip
description: Secure clipboard with optional PIN protection and end-to-end encryption. Share sensitive text safely, then let it expire.
intro: Share sensitive content with PIN locks and optional encryption — then let it disappear automatically.
paragraphs: A secure clipboard needs more than a random URL. Webklip lets you add a PIN so only people who know the code can read your content. PINs are never stored in plain text — they are hashed before saving.

For maximum privacy, enable end-to-end encryption. Your content is encrypted in the browser before it reaches the server. Only people with the decryption key in the URL can read it. The server stores ciphertext only.
Combine security with ephemerality: set a short TTL, enable burn-after-read, or cap the number of views. A secure clipboard should protect your data and then delete it when the job is done.
benefits: PIN protection — PINs are never stored in plain text
Optional end-to-end encryption in the browser
Burn after read for single-use secrets
Automatic deletion after expiry
useCases: Send a temporary password to a colleague
Share an API key for a one-time setup
Transfer sensitive notes that should not persist

title: Share Code Snippets Online — Developer Clipboard | Webklip
description: Share code snippets between devices and colleagues. Syntax highlighting, real-time sync, no chat app required.
intro: Move code between workstations with a link that syncs in real time and expires when you are done.
paragraphs: Developers constantly need to share code snippets — between their own machines or with a colleague. Pasting into Slack or email leaves permanent copies and strips formatting. Webklip gives you a temporary page with syntax highlighting and real-time sync.
Set the language for proper highlighting, paste your snippet, and share the URL. Open it on another monitor, your laptop, or send it to a colleague. Changes sync instantly if you are pair-editing.
When the review is done, the clip expires — no snippet graveyard in your chat history.
benefits: Syntax highlighting for dozens of languages
Real-time sync for collaborative editing
Automatic expiry — no permanent chat logs
Works in any modern browser — no install required
useCases: Move a function between your desktop and laptop
Share a config file with a colleague for review
Paste a quick fix while pair-editing

title: Burn After Read Links — Self-Destructing Clips | Webklip
description: Create burn after read links that delete themselves after a single view. Perfect for one-time secrets and passwords.
intro: Share a secret once. After it has been viewed, the clip deletes itself permanently.
paragraphs: Some information should only be seen once — a password, a recovery code, or a temporary credential. Burn after read links delete the clip automatically after a single view. The recipient gets the content, and it is gone.
Combine burn-on-read with PIN protection for an extra layer: even if someone guesses the URL, they still need the PIN. Set a short TTL as a backup so the clip expires even if nobody opens it.
Burn after read is built for one-time handoffs — share a secret, know it was consumed, and let Webklip handle cleanup.
benefits: Automatic deletion after a single view
Combine with PIN protection for sensitive secrets
Configurable TTL as a fallback expiry
No permanent copies left behind
useCases: Send a one-time password to a colleague
Share a temporary credential that self-destructs on retrieval
Deliver a recovery code that cannot be re-read

title: End-to-End Encrypted Clipboard | Webklip
description: End-to-end encrypted clipboard. Content encrypted in your browser — the server never sees plaintext.
intro: Encrypt your content in the browser before it leaves your device. Only people with the key can read it.
paragraphs: Standard clipboard sharing trusts the server with your content. Webklip's end-to-end encryption option encrypts text in your browser before sending it. The server stores ciphertext only — it cannot read your data even if compelled.
The decryption key lives in the URL fragment (the part after #), which browsers never send to the server. Share the full URL including the key, and recipients decrypt locally in their browser.

Combine encryption with automatic expiry and burn-on-read for a complete ephemeral secret-sharing workflow. Your content is encrypted in transit and at rest on the server, readable only by people you share the key with.
benefits: Encryption happens in your browser before upload
Server stores ciphertext only — no plaintext access
Decryption key stays in the URL fragment
Works alongside PIN protection and auto-expiry
useCases: Share credentials that the server cannot read
Send sensitive notes with zero-knowledge storage
Transfer encrypted content between your own devices
